mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is strongest when the rate has fallen for at least two consecutive monthly observations and fails if cuts do not coincide with higher equity returns.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average during sustained FEDFUNDS declines, or if the signal's Rank IC stays below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, policy is restrictive enough that equity multiples compress and short-horizon returns turn negative. The effect should be observable over the next 20 trading days and disappear once the policy rate falls back below the threshold.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not produce negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if more than 60% of triggered instances are positive.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income prospects and higher recession odds, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when unemployment rises materially from its recent level and fails if labor deterioration is absorbed without equity weakness.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE acceleration above 0.3pp in 3 months is followed by nonnegative median 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign accuracy falls below 55% in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A broad acceleration in GDPC1 paired with stronger RSAFS indicates real final demand is expanding, supporting earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect should fail when growth is led by inventory swings rather than consumer and sales activity.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous improvements in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not coincide with positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no predictive power versus single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: A falling INDPRO signals weakening industrial activity and softer corporate revenue momentum, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal should be clearest when industrial production decelerates across consecutive monthly readings.
failure_condition: Reject if negative monthly changes in INDPRO are not associated with below-zero average 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the relationship flips persistently for 2+ years.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST above trend marks housing demand momentum and upstream employment support, which should lift cyclical equity sentiment over the next month. The signal fails if housing starts rise while other demand indicators remain flat and equities do not respond.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakouts above the 3-month average do not precede positive 20-day SPX returns, or if fewer than 55% of breakout episodes are positive.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS falls while UNRATE rises, the labor market is deteriorating in a way that should compress earnings expectations and weaken S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This joint condition is stronger than either series alone and fails if employment losses are offset by stable unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-down and UNRATE-up condition does not predict negative 20-day SPX returns better than either series separately, or if interaction IC is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling CPIAUCSL and easing FEDFUNDS after a tightening episode supports real disposable income and lower discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism should activate only after inflation has stopped accelerating and policy has begun to loosen.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change < prior 3-month annualized change
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation plus easing does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if triggered episodes have negative median returns for two consecutive years.
